title: Tangy Creamy Irish Soda Bread Gratin
description: A twist on traditional Irish soda bread, featuring a tangy BBQ sauce and caramelized top. Perfect for a weekend brunch.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, and coarse salt.
Add the cold butter and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Mix in the soda bread loaf, crumbled into 1-inch pieces.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the yogurt, egg, and a pinch of black pepper.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes, until the dough is smooth and elastic.
Form the dough into a round loaf and place it in a greased 9x5-inch loaf pan.
Bake the loaf for 35-40 minutes, or until it is golden brown and sounds hollow when tapped on the bottom.
While the bread is baking, mix the barbecue sauce and a pinch of black pepper in a small bowl.
Once the bread is done, let it cool for 10 minutes before transferring it to a serving dish.
Drizzle the BBQ sauce mixture over the top of the bread, then sprinkle with chopped fresh chives.
Place the bread under the broiler for 2-3 minutes, or until the top is caramelized and golden brown.
Remove the bread from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving
